LAW AND MOTION 302, THE MATTER WAS NOT ARGUED. THE COURT ADOPTED ITS TENTATIVE RULING. CROSS DEFENDANTS YUE WANG, MAN GUAN, WILLIE YEE, JERRY LO AND MG BEAUTY CARE'S DEMURRER TO 2ND AMENDED CROSS COMPLAINT OF NATURAL WONDER LLC IS OVERRULED IN ITS ENTIRETY. THE FIRST, SECOND, FOURTH AND FIFTH CLAIMS ARE BASED AT LEAST IN PART ON FACTUAL ALLEGATIONS THAT ARE DIFFERENT FROM THE ALLEGATIONS THAT ARE THE BASIS OF THE THIRD CLAIM FOR MISAPPROPRIATION OF TRADE SECRETS. WHILE CONSPIRACY IS TECHNICALLY NOT A STAND ALONE CLAIM, IT IS A BASIS TO IMPUTE LIABILITY AND THUS IT IS APPROPRIATELY ALLEGED. NOTHING OTHER THAN ADDITIONAL COST TO THE PARTIES AND DELAY WOULD BE ACHIEVED BY SUSTAINING THE DEMURRER AS TO THE SIXTH CAUSE OF ACTION WITH LEAVE TO AMEND TO ALLEGE THE CONSPIRACY ALLEGATIONS IN THE INTRODUCTORY FACTS, SO THE COURT DECLINES TO DO SO. ORDER SIGNED IN OPEN COURT. JUDGE: HAROLD KAHN; NOT REPORTED.

Off Calendar Mar-29-2012 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:

APPEARANCES - ATTY. MEGAN JEANNE OF THE CORPORATE LAW GROUP, TEL. MO. (650) 227-8000 APPEARING FOR THE DEFENDANTS NATURAL WONDER & FU & LIU;
NO OTHER APPEARANCES ON THE MATTER.

RULING - THE MATTER WAS NOT ARGUED. The Court adopted its tentative ruling. CROSS DEFENDANTS YUE WANG, MAN GUAN, WILLIE YEE, JERRY LO and MG BEAUTY CARE'S DEMURRER TO 2ND AMENDED CROSS COMPLAINT OF NATURAL WONDER LLC is Overruled in its entirety. The first, second, fourth and fifth claims are based at least in part on factual allegations that are different from the allegations that are the basis of the third claim for misappropriation of trade secrets. While conspiracy is technically not a stand alone claim, it is a basis to impute liability and thus it is appropriately alleged. Nothing other than additional cost to the parties and delay would be achieved by sustaining the demurrer as to the sixth cause of action with leave to amend to allege the conspiracy allegations in the introductory facts, so the court declines to do so. ORDER SIGNED IN OPEN COURT. Judge: Harold Kahn; Not reported. =(302/HEK)
